heading: Application and Client-Side Best Practices
body:
You can configure features of your MongoDB deployments and the driver libraries
to create a resilient application that can withstand network outages and
failover events. To write application code that takes full advantage of the
always-on capabilities of MongoDB Atlas, you should perform the following
tasks:

- [Install the latest client libraries](resilient-install-drivers).

- [Use the connection string provided by Atlas](resilient-connection-strings).

- [single default retry](resilient-retryable-writes-reads).

- Use a `majority` [write concern and a read concern](resilient-write-read-concern) that makes sense
  for your application.

- [handle the error](resilient-error-handling) in your application.
